target.name: Pickle Robot Company
target.founder: AJ Meyer, Ariana Eisenstein, Dan Paluska

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $113M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Pickle Robot Company is a Series B robotics and manufacturing firm that has raised $113M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable, all operating in robotics and manufacturing at equivalent or adjacent stages. The peer universe spans valuations from $300M to $10.0B, with representative companies including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), and Dexterity ($1.6B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $113M in total raised as a proxy for current value, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage progression, yielding $215M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables, identifying a median valuation of $1.1B. The wide dispersion among peers—from $300M (Fairmat, Collaborative Robotics) to $10.0B (1X Technologies)—reflects material heterogeneity in market positioning and capital efficiency within the robotics sector.

The triangulated range is $182M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $645M. This wide band reflects tension between the conservative stage-progression method and the peer-median approach. Key uncertainties include unit economics, customer concentration, deployment scale relative to peers, and whether the target tracks closer to lower-valuation comparables or outliers like 1X. Additional diligence on capital deployment efficiency and revenue traction would narrow this range.
